retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Equipment -- Resin-based sealants
retrieved_docs.content: Resin-based sealants are considered the first-line treatment due to their superior retention and mechanical properties. They typically consist of an organic resin matrix, eg, Bis-GMA, UDMA, or TEGDMA, and may include fillers, fluoride-releasing agents, or photo-initiators to enhance anticariogenic properties and physical performance. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Laser Erbium-Yag Resurfacing -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: The coagulation capability of the Er:YAG laser - sometimes also known as "CO2 emulation" because of the CO2 laser's tendency to coagulate tissue - allows for greater skin tightening with reduced bleeding. When multiple passes are used, the first pass is done in the "ablate" mode to remove the superficial layers of the epidermis and dermis and expose the underlying collagen in the dermis. Subsequent passes in the "coagulation" mode can achieve greater depth of ablation and thermal injury to the upper and mid-papillary dermis. [15] When full field ablation is performed, and to a lesser extent when using a fractionated beam, it is advisable to hold a smoke evacuator with a ULPA filter within 2 cm of the tissue being ablated to prevent inhalation of smoke that may contain aerosolized microorganisms, such as viruses; this is particularly important when ablating skin lesions, such as papillomas. Pinpoint bleeding generally ensues after either fractionated or full field ablation; nonadherent gauze soaked in a vasoconstrictor, such as oxymetazoline or epinephrine, can be very helpful for controlling blood loss.

retrieved_docs.title: Urrets-Zavalia Syndrome -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Urrets-Zavalia syndrome may be associated with intracameral air or gas, particularly in procedures such as DSAEK and DALK with Descemet membrane perforation, highlighting the need for cautious use of these agents and consideration of intravenous mannitol. [23] The role of preoperative antiglaucoma medications in preventing UZS remains uncertain. Controversy surrounds the use of postoperative mydriatics, which some authors speculate may contribute to the onset of Urrets-Zavalia syndrome. Recommendations include avoiding potent mydriatics such as atropine or homatropine and considering the use of short-acting agents such as phenylephrine on alternate days. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Episiotomy -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Obstetric anal sphincter injuries : Regional or general anesthesia may be required. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Equipment -- Glass ionomer sealants
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer sealants provide a valuable alternative when resin placement is contraindicated, eg, in partially erupted molars or areas with poor isolation. These materials release fluoride over time, supporting long-term caries prevention. [18] [19] Conventional glass ionomers set through an acid-base reaction between fluoroaluminosilicate glass and polyacrylic acid, while resin-modified glass ionomers incorporate monomers, eg, 2-hydroxyethyl methacrylate (HEMA) or urethane dimethacrylate (UDMA), undergoing both acid-base and light-initiated polymerization to improve physical properties. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Episiotomy -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Monofilament sutures : Often preferred due to a reduced risk of infection. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography Endobronchial Assessment, Protocols, and Interpretation -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: A vacuum lock suction syringe – this extracts sample material under negative pressure into a sealed system.

retrieved_docs.title: Episiotomy -- Technique or Treatment -- Types of Episiotomy
retrieved_docs.content: Modified-median episiotomy A variation of the median episiotomy. Two transverse incisions are added bilaterally, just above the anal sphincter, extending the median incision. The transverse cuts are perpendicular to the midline, with a total length of 2 to 5 cm. This approach aims to increase the vaginal outlet diameter by about 83% compared to a standard median episiotomy. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Episiotomy -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: When performing an episiotomy and its subsequent repair, the following equipment and preparation are necessary:

retrieved_docs.title: Excimer Laser Coronary Angioplasty -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Excimer laser coronary angioplasty (ELCA) equipment includes an excimer laser-generator and catheters of variable sizes capable of delivering this laser energy. There is currently only one cardiovascular laser system approved for use in the United States by the FDA. [16] It generates a pulsed XeCl laser energy beam, which has a wavelength of 308 mm in the UV–B range, tissue depth of penetration between 0-30 µm, outflow range of 30 to 80 mJ/mm (termed fluence), repetition rate between 25 to 80 pulses per second (Hz), and a duration (termed pulse width) range of 125 to 200 ns.

retrieved_docs.title: Urrets-Zavalia Syndrome -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Although penetrating keratoplasty is the most common implicated procedure for this syndrome, [5] UZS has been reported following various ophthalmic surgical procedures, including deep anterior lamellar keratoplasty (DALK), [6] Descemet stripping automated endothelial keratoplasty (DSAEK), [7] cataract surgery, [8] and various glaucoma surgeries. [9] [10] [11] Patients usually present with an atonic pupil that does not constrict to light or accommodation. [12] The onset of symptoms varies but commonly occurs 1 to 2 weeks after penetrating keratoplasty. [13]

retrieved_docs.title: Excimer Laser Coronary Angioplasty -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: ELCA catheters are currently available in four diameters (0.9 mm, 1.4 mm, 1.7 mm, and 2.0 mm), and are of two types based on the arrangement of fiberoptic fibers within the catheter. They can either be concentric around the guidewire lumen or eccentrically localized towards one hemisphere. Most commonly, the 0.9 mm catheter is used. Catheters can be conventional over-the-wire or newer rapid-exchange/monorail catheters. Monorail catheters are currently being utilized more commonly, given their advantages of better tip control and axial force transmission.

retrieved_docs.title: Postcoital Contraception -- Function -- Methods Available
retrieved_docs.content: Based on the available literature, EC has been in use since 1960 and originally consisted of a high dose of oral estrogen, which soon was phased out due to increased side effects. [7] Copper intrauterine devices (IUD) and Yuzpe methods became popular in the 1970s. The first dedicated U.S Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approval for the product for EC was in 1998. [6] Several products have been introduced since that time. The oral hormonal method and the non-hormonal copper intrauterine device (Cu-IUD) are the two available groups for emergency contraception for women to avoid unintended pregnancy. Levonorgestrel (LNG), ulipristal acetate (UPA), and Yuzpe method using combined estrogen-progestin pills are the 3 currently approved oral hormonal methods in the United States. Mifepristone, which acts by inhibiting progesterone, is available for use as an emergency contraceptive in other countries. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Postcoital Contraception -- Function -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: Copper IUD releases copper ion that is toxic to sperm and oocytes and thereby inhibits fertilization. In addition, the inflammatory response in the endometrium due to Cu-IUD is hostile for the fertilized zygote and potentially inhibits implantation. It is the most effective of all the methods and has additional ongoing contraceptive benefits for up to 10 years after insertion. [8] [9] All oral emergency contraceptives work principally either by inhibiting or delaying ovulation. Sperm survives and remains fertile for up to 5 days in the female reproductive tract, and oocyte stays fertile for 24 hrs. When ovulation is delayed by 5 days, the contact between sperm and oocyte is avoided, and fertilization does not happen. Levonorgestrel (LNG) EC inhibits ovulation by blocking the luteinizing hormone (LH) surge, which in turn prevents follicular development and rupture. Hence, it has no effect once the LH surge has begun or after ovulation. Ulipristal acetate (UPA) exerts similar effects and was found to be effective even after the onset of LH surge but before the peak. This explains the increase in the window of efficacy for UPA as EC for up to 120 hours (5 days) compared to a 72 hrs window for LNG only EC. Some post-ovulatory effects, including decreased endometrial receptivity, altered tubal function, and modulation in sperm activation, were noted in in-vitro studies for UPA with uncertain clinical relevance in the context of emergency contraception. [7] [9] Mifepristone is an oral anti-progestogen and used at a lower dose of 10 mg as EC and at higher doses as an abortifacient. It prevents or delays ovulation and affects endometrial development and implantation, explaining its efficacy even when administered in the post-ovulatory period. The Yuzpe method is the least effective method with a mechanism of action similar to the LNG only method. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Urrets-Zavalia Syndrome -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Although the etiology of UZS is not well understood, several potential risk factors mentioned below are believed to be involved in its development: Increased intraoperative or postoperative intraocular pressure (IOP) [2] Use of mydriatic agents during or after surgical intervention [18] Presence of keratoconus [2] [19] Retention of viscoelastic material in the anterior chamber angle [20] Air or gas injection into the anterior chamber [21] [22] [23] Inflammation in the anterior chamber [5] These risk factors have been debated due to conflicting data from limited reports and studies on UZS. UZS has been reported following various ophthalmic interventions, including: Penetrating keratoplasty for indications such as keratoconus, macular corneal dystrophy, granular corneal dystrophy, Fuchs endothelial corneal dystrophy, chemical injury, fungal corneal ulcer, leucoma, and herpetic keratitis. [5] [24] [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Episiotomy -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Disinfection : Use of Betadine or chlorhexidine solution to clean the perineal area before the episiotomy and the repair [2]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2125
retrieved_docs.content: Repair of episiotomy is classified as one of the other procedures associated with delivery, indicating that it falls under the broader category of delivery‑related interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1536
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy is identified as a specific therapeutic procedure within the domain of obstetric care, being categorized under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد. This surgical incision of the perineum, performed during the second stage of labor, is considered one of the specialized interventions used to facilitate vaginal delivery and protect surrounding tissues. Recognizing episiotomy as part of the broader set of obstetric therapeutic procedures underscores its role in managing childbirth complications and informs clinical decision‑making regarding when and how to employ this technique for optimal maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to fetal stimulation, reduction of multiple gestations, and surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancies—providing a comprehensive framework for coordinating obstetric care across the ante‑partum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2377
retrieved_docs.content: The female perineal structure is the direct procedural site of a low forceps delivery with episiotomy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 2261
retrieved_docs.content: Preventive incision is directly related to episiotomy, indicating that a preventive incision is considered a form or aspect of the episiotomy procedure. Episiotomy itself is categorized under the broader group of obstetric therapeutic procedures, referred to in Arabic as الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد, which encompasses medical interventions performed during childbirth to facilitate delivery and protect maternal and fetal health. This relationship highlights that preventive incisions are employed within the context of episiotomy as part of specialized obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2635
retrieved_docs.content: Manual conversion of position is a medical procedure whose site is the uterus, indicating that it is performed on or involves the uterine organ. Endometriosis is a disease that is located in the uterus, reflecting the presence of ectopic endometrial tissue within or on the uterine structure. Both concepts highlight the uterus as a central focus for therapeutic interventions and pathological conditions, underscoring the importance of uterine health in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2255
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures associated with delivery are classified as subcategories within the broader group of other procedures to assist delivery and also fall under the specific category of repair of episiotomy, indicating that repair of episiotomy is considered one of the other procedures associated with delivery and that these interventions are part of the overall set of procedures used to assist childbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1790
retrieved_docs.content: Routine obstetric care, which includes comprehensive antepartum care, vaginal delivery (with or without episiotomy and/or forceps), and postpartum care, is broadly related to assisted delivery techniques such as forceps, vacuum extraction, and breech delivery, highlighting the integration of standard obstetric management with these specialized delivery methods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 2315
retrieved_docs.content: Intrauterine pregnancy is clinically associated with الولادة بالملقط and الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ. The presence of an intrauterine pregnancy may increase the likelihood of requiring a forceps delivery (الولادة بالملقط) due to obstetric complications, and it also predisposes to inflammation of the uterine lining (الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ), which can affect maternal outcomes. Recognizing these associations helps clinicians anticipate potential delivery interventions and manage the risk of postpartum endometritis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 1 -- Community 137
retrieved_docs.content: delivery by vacuum extraction, and delivery of a dead fetus. Repair of episiotomy and other obstetric repairs are placed under OBSTETRICS: SURGICAL REPAIRS, CLOSURES AND RECONSTRUCTIONS, a subcategory of obstetric surgical procedures. Overall, the interconnected hierarchy links routine obstetric care, assisted‑delivery techniques, surgical repairs, and life‑threatening complications, providing a comprehensive framework for precise clinical decision‑making and patient management throughout the full scope of childbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1532
retrieved_docs.content: Other complications of pregnancy are uterine conditions that arise within the uterus and are clinically associated with optional termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري). This relationship underscores that these complications are localized to the uterine environment and may influence or be influenced by decisions regarding elective pregnancy termination, highlighting the importance of uterine health in the management of pregnancy‑related problems.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 2 -- Community 1049
retrieved_docs.content: tying membrane‑related techniques together. Finally, other procedures associated with delivery is also linked to repair of episiotomy as a parent, indicating that episiotomy repair is considered part of the suite of ancillary delivery procedures. Together, these relationships illustrate a structured network in which various labor‑induction and delivery‑assistance interventions are organized, interrelated, and sometimes overlapping, providing a comprehensive framework for understanding and managing assisted vaginal delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 2516
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery constitute a broad category of obstetric interventions that are employed to facilitate vaginal birth. Within this category, the specific complication فشل التوليد بالملقط (failure of forceps delivery) is identified as a child procedure, indicating that it is a particular adverse outcome associated with assisted delivery techniques. Episiotomy, a surgical incision made in the perineum to enlarge the vaginal opening, functions as a parent procedure within the same group, linking various delivery‑assisting methods and serving as a foundational technique for related interventions. Preventive incision, exemplified by episiotomy, is recognized as having a direct relationship to these obstetric procedures, underscoring the role of episiotomy as a preventive measure designed to reduce trauma during delivery and to support the successful execution of other assisted delivery methods. Together, these concepts illustrate how episiotomy and preventive incisions are integral components of the spectrum of procedures used to assist delivery, while also highlighting specific complications such as فشل التوليد بالملقط that can arise from assisted vaginal birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.
